resources.string: An isolated Android Work Profile will be created. Apps inside it will be completely separated from your personal data.
Allow copy/paste between main and work profiles
Show work contacts when calling from the main profile
Allow installing apps from files (not from Google Play) in the work profile
Allow file sharing between profiles via \"Share\"
Trigger when any virtual network (private/corporate) is active
Trigger when connected to specified networks\n\nGPS MUST be enabled for this trigger to work!

Freeze marked apps, others go direct (e.g., when Virtual Network trigger fires, main profile apps go through it, while work profile apps bypass it) — Protect corporate networks from scanning and attacks!
When triggered, block ALL network traffic (including unmarked apps). Maximum protection mode — no app can access the internet.

CoreGuard creates an isolated workspace (Work Profile) on your device (standard Android feature for corporate users).
All work profile apps operate in corporate network protection mode by default — blocking untrusted applications!
When a trigger fires (Virtual Network, Wi-Fi, Operator):
🔴 Marked apps — completely blocked
🟢 Unmarked apps — continue working directly
You choose which apps to block, how, and under what conditions.
In settings, you can enable Kill Switch mode — then when a trigger fires, ALL network traffic will be blocked without exceptions (for work profile apps).

CoreGuard requests only one permission during installation.
This is required to determine network state and active connections in the main profile.
⚠ CoreGuard does NOT collect, NOT store, and NOT transmit any of your data. The app works entirely locally on your device.
Corporate network and device protection!\n\nAll apps below are already isolated from the main space. For maximum protection: check the apps — when a trigger fires, they will be completely frozen.
Work Profile created! Open the app with the 🎒 icon
Find the app icon with a briefcase 🎒 in the app list — this is CoreGuard\'s Work Profile. Open it to manage protection.

On MIUI (Xiaomi/Redmi/POCO) background services may be stopped.
For continuous protection:
1. Settings → Apps → CoreGuard → Autostart → Enable
2. Settings → Battery → Battery Saver → CoreGuard → No Restrictions
3. Settings → Apps → CoreGuard → Other Permissions → Run in Background → Allow

On Huawei/Honor background processes are aggressively restricted.
For continuous operation:
Settings → Battery → App Launch → CoreGuard → Manage Manually → enable all three permissions.
Note: without Google services (GMS), Android Work Profile may be unstable.

On Samsung One UI, disable battery optimization for CoreGuard:
Settings → Device Care → Battery → App Restrictions → CoreGuard → Unrestricted.

On OPPO / OnePlus / Realme / Vivo the system aggressively kills background apps.
Add CoreGuard to the autostart list and disable battery restrictions in system settings (ColorOS / OxygenOS / FunTouchOS).

Some manufacturers restrict background apps.
For reliable operation: Settings → Battery → find CoreGuard → select \"Unrestricted\".
During initial workspace setup, the phone may experience heavy load — this is normal. Wait for the process to complete, do not close the app, don\'t rush — device slowdown at this point is expected!

💡 For downloaded apps: find Play Store with the 🎒 (briefcase) icon — this is the work profile store. Install the apps there — they will be isolated in the protected space. DON\'T FORGET to remove duplicate apps from the main space after installing them in the work profile.
All available system apps are already enabled in the work profile.
No networks found. Make sure Wi-Fi is enabled.
CoreGuard v1.0 · with gratitude to Anthropic
Some apps (Contacts, Calendar, Gallery) may sync data between main and work profiles. Tap to open system settings and check.
Some apps (Contacts, Calendar, Gallery) may sync data between main and work profiles. Check in: Settings → Accounts → Work Profile.

You are in the Work Profile — all apps here are protected by CoreGuard.
For effective protection:
1. Install needed apps in this space via Play Store with the 🎒 (briefcase) icon
2. Remove these apps from the main space — this is REQUIRED for protection!
3. Mark installed apps in the block list on the main screen (for maximum protection)
⚠ If apps remain in both spaces — protection will not work for copies in the main profile.
Open CoreGuard system settings: autostart, battery, permissions. Required for background operation.

A Work Profile already exists on this device (possibly created by Shelter or another manager).
CoreGuard cannot use another app\'s Work Profile. To proceed:
1. Remove the existing Work Profile in device settings 2. Restart CoreGuard and create a new profile
📍 Path for your device: %s
⚠ Removing the Work Profile will delete all data and apps inside it!!!
Settings → Additional Settings → Accounts & Sync → Remove Work Profile
Settings → Accounts and Backup → Manage Accounts → Work Profile → Remove Work Profile
Settings → Users & Accounts → Remove Work Profile
Settings → Users & Accounts → Remove Work Profile
Settings → Passwords & Accounts → Work Profile → Remove Work Profile
All apps installed in the Work Profile are managed by CoreGuard. When a trigger fires, marked apps will be blocked.
Work profile apps cannot take screenshots or record the screen. Blocks hidden background captures of running apps. Your screenshots from the work profile will also be blocked.
Prevents apps from reading IMEI and phone number — protection against passive ID tracking. Limitations: some apps may ask you to enter SMS code manually. Android 11+
Prevents apps from determining precise and background location. Limitations: location-based apps won\'t be able to determine your position. Android 11+
Prevents apps from accessing the camera. Limitations: QR code scanning and video calls won\'t work. Android 11+
Prevents apps from accessing the microphone. Limitations: voice input and in-app calls won\'t work. Android 11+
Prevents apps from reading work profile contacts. Limitations: autofill and contact search won\'t work. Android 11+

Prevents reading and sending SMS. Protection against banking OTP code theft via READ_SMS. Limitations: auto SMS verification won\'t work — apps using SMS confirmation will ask you to enter the code manually. Android 11+
Prevents access to call history (who was called, when, duration). Limitations: minimal — rarely used legitimately. Android 11+
Prevents apps from discovering Google accounts on the device (GET_ACCOUNTS). Prevents identity tracking via email. Limitations: some apps won\'t auto-sign in with Google. Android 11+
Prevents scanning nearby BT devices (BLUETOOTH_SCAN). Prevents GPS-free geolocation via BT signatures of places (subway, office). Android 12+
Prevents apps from transferring files via Bluetooth (DISALLOW_BLUETOOTH_SHARING). Bluetooth itself continues to work.
Prevents sending data via NFC touch (Android Beam / NDEF Push). Receiving via NFC remains functional.
All work profile apps cannot be uninstalled normally. Disable this before removing unwanted apps.
Individual exceptions from global restrictions for each app

By default, each app follows the global profile settings (above). Here you can set exceptions: allow a specific app something that is globally restricted, or vice versa. Modified apps are highlighted.

CoreGuard creates an isolated workspace (Work Profile) on your device — a standard Android feature for corporate users.
🛡️ How it works: Untrusted apps must not operate when the device has access to the corporate network. CoreGuard controls this automatically.
🔐 Combined trigger system: • Virtual Network — detect corporate connection • Wi-Fi — bind to specific corporate networks (SSID) • Mobile Internet — control when switching to cellular • Operator — react to a specific mobile operator
⚡ When a trigger fires: 🔴 Marked apps — completely blocked 🟢 Unmarked — continue working (depending on your settings!)
In Kill Switch mode, ALL network traffic is blocked without exceptions.
📲 CoreGuard does NOT collect, NOT store, and NOT transmit your data. Everything works locally on the device.

You are in the Work Profile — all apps here are managed by CoreGuard.
🛡️ Zero Trust Principle: Untrusted apps must not operate when the device has corporate network access. CoreGuard controls this automatically through a combination of triggers (Virtual Network + Wi-Fi + Mobile + Operator).
📲 For effective protection: 1. Install needed apps via Play Store with the 🎒 (briefcase) icon (Work Profile) 2. Remove these apps from the main space — REQUIRED 3. Mark apps for blocking on the main screen and configure corporate network parameters in settings!
⚡ When a trigger fires: 🔴 Marked apps — completely blocked 🟢 Unmarked — continue working (depends on your settings!)
In Kill Switch mode, ALL network traffic is blocked.
⚠ If apps remain in both spaces — protection will not work for copies in the main profile.
CoreGuard does NOT collect, NOT store, and NOT transmit your data.
Trigger when connected to a specific operator
When connected to a corporate Wi-Fi network (from the list above), automatically enable Kill Switch mode — full network block for maximum protection

If you mark this trigger as corporate — when it fires, ALL work profile apps will be completely frozen and network will be blocked (Kill Switch), regardless of main screen settings.\n\nMark as corporate network?
Mark networks that are corporate.\n\nWhen connected to a corporate network, ALL work profile apps will be frozen and network will be blocked (Kill Switch).
Mark operators whose connection is considered corporate.\n\nWhen connected through a corporate operator, ALL work profile apps will be frozen and network will be blocked (Kill Switch).
